title: GH #15: bee config get/set/unset so config is set via validated CLI, not hand-edit JSON
action: Add config CLI verbs so users/agents never hand-edit .bee/config.json (the one .bee file that violated rule-12's everything-through-CLI). New handlers in bee.mjs: `config get --key K`, `config set --key K --value V [--string]`, `config unset --key K` — dot-notation nested keys (guards.idle_gate), JSON value coercion (false->boolean, repo->string), validate-on-write refusing only NEWLY-introduced models/cli problems, refuse-on-malformed (never clobber an unparseable config), unset prunes empty parent objects. Registry entries + DA5 bijection + example-exec + 'Use:' line. Also: the intake-gate error message now teaches `bee config set --key guards.idle_gate --value false` instead of 'hand-edit the JSON' (the exact anti-pattern #15 objects to, shown in the reporter's transcript). Documented in docs/config-reference.md. NOTE: this is #2 of the two-part plan; #1 (exempt git/VCS ops from the intake gate) deferred for careful discussion per user.

truths: config get/set/unset work with dot-notation nested keys and JSON value coercion.
set refuses to write a newly-invalid models/cli config or overwrite an unparseable file; unset prunes empty parents.
The intake-gate message points to the CLI, not a hand-edit.
artifacts: Full 21-suite verify green after mirror sync + manifest regenerate.
test_bee_cli: round-trip + nested + coercion + refuse-on-invalid + no-clobber + prune; DA5 bijection + example-exec green.
prohibitions: Do NOT loosen the intake gate for git (that is #1, deferred).
Do NOT make config.json CLI-owned/blocked for hand-edit in this change (separate decision).

trace.outcome: bee config get/set/unset CLI so config is set through a validated CLI (dot-keys, JSON coercion, refuse-on-invalid, no-clobber, prune) instead of hand-editing JSON; intake-gate message teaches the CLI
trace.verification_evidence:
Before (GitHub #15): .bee/config.json was the ONE .bee file with no CLI — every field (product_root, models, commands, guards.idle_gate, gate_bypass) was hand-edited JSON, violating rule-12's everything-through-CLI. The reporter's transcript shows an agent hand-editing config.json to add "guards":{"idle_gate":false} to run a git deploy during a closed phase, then re-enabling it — and the intake-gate error message itself INSTRUCTED that hand-edit.
After: bee config get/set/unset --key K [--value V] — dot-notation nested keys, JSON value coercion (false->boolean, repo->string, --string forces string), validate-on-write refusing only NEWLY-introduced models/cli problems, refuse-on-malformed (never clobbers an unparseable file), unset prunes empty parents. The intake-gate message now teaches `bee config set --key guards.idle_gate --value false`.
Evidence: test_bee_cli 145/0 — round-trip set(false->bool)/get/unset; nested guards.idle_gate set + unset-prunes-empty-parent; product_root stays string "repo"; unsafe cli set REFUSED and not written; malformed file left intact on refused set; DA5 bijection (Use: get,set,unset,validate) + every-example-executed green. Full 21-suite foreground CHAIN_EXIT:0; mirrors byte-identical. Scope: this is part #2; #1 (exempt git/VCS from the intake gate) deferred per user.
trace.verify_output: test_bee_cli 145/0 incl 2 new config tests (round-trip set/get/unset + nested dot-key/coercion/refuse-on-invalid/no-clobber/prune) + DA5 bijection (config group verbs get/set/unset/validate) + every-example-executed; test_lib_mirror byte-identical; release_manifest --check 141. Full 21-suite chain foreground CHAIN_EXIT:0 (the earlier background red was a concurrency artifact: my poll-command hooks wrote live .bee/cache/inject-cache.json while hook_contracts' live-state fingerprint ran — passes clean without concurrency).
